note: The waterfront monument sits on a large flat paved plaza that is step-free to approach and photograph, though extremely crowded and with security checks. The plaza itself is easy.
The iconic seafront promenade is flat, paved and largely step-free for its length, with the famous curve and sunset views; easy for wheeling and strolling. Some kerbs.

Reached by a ferry then a long steep staircase (or a small toy train and more steps) up to rock-cut cave temples; no accessible route to the caves. Not suitable for wheelchairs. Alt: the harbour ferry ride itself for the views.
The major museum has ramped access and lifts to most galleries with an accessible entrance and loaner wheelchair on request; grand grounds are largely flat. Partly to largely accessible.
The UNESCO Victorian-Gothic station is stunning to view from the flat plaza outside, but as a working station the interior is crowded and stepped. Admire the exterior; interior tough.
A flat seaside promenade in trendy Bandra, largely step-free with sea views and Bollywood-star homes nearby; easy strolling though busy at sunset.
The mosque-tomb sits on an islet reached by a long narrow causeway that floods at high tide and is crowded and uneven; ramps are being improved but access is hard and tidal. Verify conditions.
Bustling markets that are flat but crowded, with uneven floors, thresholds and tight lanes; navigable slowly but a real squeeze for wheels and strollers.
The terraced hilltop gardens have some flat paved paths and viewpoints though a few steps and slopes; the flatter sections are pleasant and accessible.
A modern museum complex with ramps and lifts and step-free galleries; good accessible option for families away from the crowds.
A famous, lively beach; the promenade edges are flat but the sand itself is soft and not wheelchair-friendly without a beach chair. Promenade manageable; beach hard.
